title: Ixora expandens
taxonomy: Ixora expandens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Ixora, which contains over 400 species of plants native to tropical regions. Ixora expandens is native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in India and Sri Lanka. The plant is often used as an ornamental in gardens and is valued for its vibrant red flowers. Ixora expandens is a small shrub that typically grows to a height of 1-2 meters.

intro: Ixora coccinea, also known as Ixora expandens, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and folk medicine, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various conditions such as fever, cold, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Ixora coccinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ixora coccinea is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to make herbal teas, tonics, and ointments.
